verses.text: Seeing the multitudes, He goes up the mountain and teaches His disciples. The great sermon. The King opens His mouth to set forth the laws of His kingdom.
Seeing the multitudes, He goes up the mountain and teaches His disciples. The great sermon. The King opens His mouth to set forth the laws of His kingdom.
'Blessed are the poor in spirit... they that mourn... the meek'; theirs are the kingdom, comfort, and the earth. Surprising blessedness. God's favour rests on the lowly, not the proud.
'Blessed are the poor in spirit... they that mourn... the meek'; theirs are the kingdom, comfort, and the earth. Surprising blessedness. God's favour rests on the lowly, not the proud.
'Blessed are the poor in spirit... they that mourn... the meek'; theirs are the kingdom, comfort, and the earth. Surprising blessedness. God's favour rests on the lowly, not the proud.

Blessed are they who hunger for righteousness, the merciful, the pure in heart, the peacemakers; they are filled, find mercy, see God, are called His sons. Inward graces. True blessing is a matter of the heart.

Blessed are they who hunger for righteousness, the merciful, the pure in heart, the peacemakers; they are filled, find mercy, see God, are called His sons. Inward graces. True blessing is a matter of the heart.

Blessed are they who hunger for righteousness, the merciful, the pure in heart, the peacemakers; they are filled, find mercy, see God, are called His sons. Inward graces. True blessing is a matter of the heart.

Blessed are they who hunger for righteousness, the merciful, the pure in heart, the peacemakers; they are filled, find mercy, see God, are called His sons. Inward graces. True blessing is a matter of the heart.
Blessed are the persecuted for righteousness; 'rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven.' Joy in suffering. Persecution for Christ is cause for gladness, not despair.
Blessed are the persecuted for righteousness; 'rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven.' Joy in suffering. Persecution for Christ is cause for gladness, not despair.
Blessed are the persecuted for righteousness; 'rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven.' Joy in suffering. Persecution for Christ is cause for gladness, not despair.
Ye are 'the salt of the earth' and 'the light of the world'; let your light shine, that men glorify your Father. Visible holiness. The Christian's good life points others to God.
Ye are 'the salt of the earth' and 'the light of the world'; let your light shine, that men glorify your Father. Visible holiness. The Christian's good life points others to God.

He came not to destroy the law but to fulfil it; unless righteousness exceed the scribes', none enters the kingdom. Heart-righteousness. God looks deeper than outward rule-keeping.
He came not to destroy the law but to fulfil it; unless righteousness exceed the scribes', none enters the kingdom. Heart-righteousness. God looks deeper than outward rule-keeping.
He came not to destroy the law but to fulfil it; unless righteousness exceed the scribes', none enters the kingdom. Heart-righteousness. God looks deeper than outward rule-keeping.
He came not to destroy the law but to fulfil it; unless righteousness exceed the scribes', none enters the kingdom. Heart-righteousness. God looks deeper than outward rule-keeping.
Not only killing, but anger, breaks the law; first 'be reconciled to thy brother,' then bring thy gift. Reconciliation first. God values a mended relationship above an offering.
Not only killing, but anger, breaks the law; first 'be reconciled to thy brother,' then bring thy gift. Reconciliation first. God values a mended relationship above an offering.

Not only killing, but anger, breaks the law; first 'be reconciled to thy brother,' then bring thy gift. Reconciliation first. God values a mended relationship above an offering.
Agree quickly with thine adversary, lest he hand thee to the judge. Settle disputes. Wisdom makes peace before matters grow worse.
Agree quickly with thine adversary, lest he hand thee to the judge. Settle disputes. Wisdom makes peace before matters grow worse.
Not only adultery, but the lustful look, is sin; better to pluck out the eye than be cast into hell. Inward purity. Christ traces sin to the desires of the heart.
Not only adultery, but the lustful look, is sin; better to pluck out the eye than be cast into hell. Inward purity. Christ traces sin to the desires of the heart.

To put away a wife, save for fornication, makes her an adulteress. Marriage honoured. Christ guards the sacredness of the marriage bond.
To put away a wife, save for fornication, makes her an adulteress. Marriage honoured. Christ guards the sacredness of the marriage bond.
Swear not at all; 'let your communication be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay.' Plain truth. The Christian's word should be so honest it needs no oath.
Swear not at all; 'let your communication be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay.' Plain truth. The Christian's word should be so honest it needs no oath.

Resist not evil; turn the other cheek; give to him that asks. Yielding love. The kingdom's way answers wrong with grace, not revenge.
Resist not evil; turn the other cheek; give to him that asks. Yielding love. The kingdom's way answers wrong with grace, not revenge.
Resist not evil; turn the other cheek; give to him that asks. Yielding love. The kingdom's way answers wrong with grace, not revenge.

Resist not evil; turn the other cheek; give to him that asks. Yielding love. The kingdom's way answers wrong with grace, not revenge.
'Love your enemies, bless them that curse you,' that ye may be children of your Father, who sends rain on just and unjust. God-like love. We resemble God when we love even foes.
'Love your enemies, bless them that curse you,' that ye may be children of your Father, who sends rain on just and unjust. God-like love. We resemble God when we love even foes.
'Love your enemies, bless them that curse you,' that ye may be children of your Father, who sends rain on just and unjust. God-like love. We resemble God when we love even foes.
To love only friends is no more than the publicans do; 'Be ye therefore perfect, even as your Father... is perfect.' The high calling. God sets His own love as our standard.
